article_title: FDA Reverses Course on UniQure's Huntington's Gene Therapy, Demanding Sham Surgery Trial in Setback That Could Add Years to Approval Timeline

content_preview.title: FDA Reverses Course on UniQure's Huntington's Gene Therapy, Demanding Sham Surgery Trial in Setback That Could Add Years to Approval Timeline
content_preview.summary: The FDA rejected uniQure's Phase I/II data for AMT-130 and now demands a sham surgery-controlled Phase 3 trial, reversing its own prior guidance and sending shares down 40 percent.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has reversed its prior regulatory guidance on uniQure's AMT-130, a one-time gene therapy for Huntington's disease, telling the company that its Phase I/II trial data are insufficient to support a marketing application and demanding instead a prospective, randomized, double-blind trial in which control patients would undergo sham brain surgery. The announcement, disclosed by [uniQure on March 2](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/03/...

recommendations: Consider adding biospace.com to the source allowlist — established biotech/pharma news outlet

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent News article with clear structure. Technical content (gene therapy mechanism, trial design, biomarker data) is explained accessibly without dumbing down. At 1197 words, within News range. The 'What We Don't Know' section adds valuable transparency.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 sources verified: two GlobeNewsWire press releases (primary sources from uniQure itself), STAT News (premier health/biotech outlet), BioSpace (established biotech news), BioPharma Dive (industry publication). Strong primary sourcing with the company's own regulatory disclosures.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Clinical data figures (75% slowing, cUHDRS -0.38 vs -1.52, p=0.003, TFC 60% slowing p=0.033, CSF NfL -8.2%) are attributed to uniQure's published results. The article accurately notes Makary's anatomical description discrepancy (intrathecal/ventricular vs intraparenchymal/striatal delivery) — a genuinely important factual detail. Stock decline figures attributed to BioSpace.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and balanced. Presents both the FDA's position and uniQure's arguments without taking sides. The 'Sham Surgery Dilemma' section presents the ethical tension fairly. The Makary section notes the discrepancy factually without editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: No existing articles on uniQure/AMT-130 or FDA gene therapy regulatory decisions. Fresh topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality News submission covering an important regulatory development in gene therapy. Strong primary sourcing, accurate clinical data reporting, and balanced presentation of the ethical and regulatory tensions. Ready for publication.
